content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN, and an integer KKK.

You can perform the following operation:


Choose any index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), and increase AiA_iAi​ by KKK.
Find the **minimum** possible value of max⁡(A)−min⁡(A)\max(A) - \min(A)max(A)−min(A) attainable, if you can perform this operation as many times as you like (possibly, zero times).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the parameter KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the initial values of the array elements.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the minimum possible value of max⁡(A)−min⁡(A)\max(A) - \min(A)max(A)−min(A) if you can perform the given operation any number of times.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Increase the first element by K=4K = 4K=4 to obtain the array [5,5,4][5, 5, 4][5,5,4].
Here, max⁡−min⁡=5−4=1\max - \min = 5-4 = 1max−min=5−4=1, which is the best possible.
Test case 222: The second and third elements can be increased by 222 till they reach 121212, at which point all the elements of the array are equal, so max⁡(A)−min⁡(A)=0\max(A) - \min(A) = 0max(A)−min(A)=0.
Test case 333: Since K=1K = 1K=1, again it's possible to make all the elements equal.
Test case 444: Do the following:

Increase A1A_1A1​ by 121212 repeatedly to make it 133133133.
Increase A2A_2A2​ by 121212 repeatedly to make it 134134134.
Increase A3A_3A3​ by 121212 repeatedly to make it 136136136.

The array is now [133,134,136,128,130,131][133, 134, 136, 128, 130, 131][133,134,136,128,130,131].
For this array, max⁡(A)−min⁡(A)=136−128=8\max(A) - \min(A) = 136 - 128 = 8max(A)−min(A)=136−128=8.
It can be shown that this is optimal.
